Title: Phytochrome B and REVEILLE1/2-mediated signalling controls seed dormancy and germination in Arabidopsis

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: 35S constitutive promoter (GA3ox2-OX in RVE1-OX background); native GA3ox2 promoter in LUC reporter
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Expressed in imbibed seeds; de-repressed under phyB-on conditions when RVE1 is reduced
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: GA precursors converted at the 3β-hydroxylation step to bioactive GAs
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: Bioactive gibberellins including GA1 and GA4
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyses a late 3-oxidation step in gibberellin biosynthesis that produces bioactive GAs
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key control point supported in this study for seed GA accumulation downstream of RVE1
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Promotes seed germination and represses dormancy
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes bioactive GA accumulation and germination; overexpression rescues the dormant/non-germinating phenotype caused by RVE1 overexpression
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GA3ox2 transcript was drastically reduced (~13-fold) in RVE1-OX seeds. 2. RVE1 directly binds the GA3ox2 promoter and represses its transcription. 3. GA3ox2 overexpression complemented both dormancy and germination defects of RVE1-OX. 4. Changes in GA3ox2 expression correlated with altered GA1 and GA4 levels in seeds.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qRT-PCR; dexamethasone-inducible nuclear translocation assay; transient promoter-LUC assay; ChIP; EMSA; transgenic overexpression rescue; metabolite profiling of GA1/GA4
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Public seed developmental expression data repositories used for expression pattern evaluation; accession NA
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Seed imbibition; red/far-red light (phyB-on/off); white light; exogenous GA3
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Manipulating GA3ox2 expression could tune seed dormancy/germination balance and may help control pre-harvest sprouting versus uniform germination
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Excess GA3ox2 activity may reduce desirable dormancy and increase risk of pre-harvest sprouting
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper establishes GA3ox2 as the direct downstream metabolic target of RVE1 in seeds.

Regulation_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: Col; Ler; Ws; Kl; Cvi (expression/dormancy comparison across ecotypes)
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: 35S constitutive promoter (RVE1-OX; 35S:Myc-RVE1; 35S:RVE1-GR); native RVE1 promoter in RVE1p:GUS
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Transcript increases during seed development and rapidly decreases after imbibition
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: High in dormant/freshly harvested seeds; positively correlated with dormancy depth among ecotypes; repressed by active phyB/red light after imbibition
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Gibberellin biosynthesis controlling seed dormancy and germination
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Major negative regulator linking phyB light signaling to suppression of bioactive GA production in seeds
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: phyB light signaling; red/far-red light status; imbibition; likely PIF1/PIF6-mediated input discussed
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: Mutual positive transcriptional relationship with DOG1 suggested; exact mechanism not fully resolved
Regulation_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: DOG1 (genetic mutual promotion reported at transcript level); phyB acts upstream genetically
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes primary seed dormancy and represses red/far-red-light-mediated germination by lowering bioactive GA biosynthesis
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. rve1 mutants germinate more readily and show reduced dormancy, whereas RVE1 overexpression strongly enhances dormancy and blocks germination. 2. RVE1 acts genetically downstream of phyB in dormancy and germination control. 3. RVE1 directly binds EE-expand motifs in the GA3ox2 promoter and represses GA3ox2 transcription. 4. rve1 seeds accumulate more GA1 and GA4, while RVE1-OX seeds have undetectable GA1/GA4. 5. GA3 treatment or GA3ox2 overexpression rescues the RVE1-OX germination defect.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Loss-of-function mutant; overexpression; double-mutant genetics with phyB and ga1; transgenic rescue with GA3ox2-OX; qRT-PCR; GUS reporter; inducible GR assay; transient LUC assay; ChIP; EMSA; GA metabolite quantification; exogenous GA3 rescue
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: Public seed developmental expression data repositories used; accession NA
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Red/far-red light pulses; white light; darkness; imbibition; cold stratification; dexamethasone; cycloheximide; exogenous GA3
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate regulator to tune seed dormancy strength and germination timing for agronomic optimization
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Overexpression causes excessive dormancy and poor germination; reducing RVE1 may decrease dormancy and risk pre-harvest sprouting
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: RVE1 also negatively affects phyA-dependent germination according to supplementary data and may correspond to dormancy QTL DOG4.

Regulation_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: Col; Ler; Ws; Kl; Cvi (expression/dormancy comparison across ecotypes)
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Transcript increases during seed development and decreases after imbibition
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Higher in more dormant ecotypes and repressed by active phyB/red light
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Transcriptional regulation; exact direct target not experimentally demonstrated here
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Acts similarly to RVE1 in repressing germination-associated GA pathway output; direct target not shown
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Additive negative regulator of bioactive GA-associated germination output downstream of phyB
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: phyB light signaling; red/far-red light status; imbibition
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes dormancy and represses phyB-mediated germination additively with RVE1
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. rve2 mutant seeds showed increased germination and reduced dormancy. 2. rve1 rve2 double mutants had stronger germination phenotypes than either single mutant. 3. RVE2 overexpression reduced phyB-mediated germination and enhanced dormancy. 4. RVE2 transcript is repressed by phyB and correlates positively with dormancy depth among ecotypes.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Loss-of-function mutant; overexpression; double-mutant analysis; qRT-PCR
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: Public seed developmental expression data repositories used; accession NA
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Red/far-red light pulses; white light; darkness; imbibition
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential combinatorial target with RVE1 to fine-tune dormancy/germination traits
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Reduced RVE2 may weaken dormancy; overexpression may impair stand establishment
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Likely corresponds to dormancy QTL DOG7 according to discussion, but not experimentally proven here.

Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: 35S constitutive promoter (35S:Myc-PHYB); PHYB-ABO transgenic overexpression line
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: phyB apoprotein accumulates during seed maturation as discussed
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Active Pfr form generated by red light after imbibition represses downstream dormancy genes
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Light-activated signaling leading to indirect transcriptional repression of downstream genes
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Seed dormancy/germination via control of GA biosynthesis regulators
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Active phyB represses expression of RVE1, RVE2 and DOG1
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Upstream light sensor that relieves dormancy and promotes germination by de-repressing GA biosynthesis
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Red light activates; far-red light inactivates; imbibition
Regulation_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: PIF1 (background/known); RVE1 and DOG1 downstream genetically
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Suppresses primary seed dormancy and promotes red/far-red-light-reversible germination upstream of RVE1/2 and DOG1
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Freshly harvested phyB mutant seeds are strongly dormant and post-harvest phyB seeds germinate poorly. 2. PHYB overexpression breaks dormancy and increases germination even in darkness. 3. phyB acts genetically upstream of RVE1 and DOG1. 4. Active phyB represses RVE1, RVE2 and DOG1 transcript accumulation.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Mutant analysis; overexpression lines; double-mutant genetics; qRT-PCR; GUS reporter; ChIP negative evidence for direct promoter binding
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Red and far-red light pulses; darkness; white light; imbibition
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Manipulation of phyB signaling could modulate dormancy and germination responses to environmental light cues
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Strong dormancy reduction may favor pre-harvest sprouting under some environments
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: phyB did not bind RVE1/RVE2/DOG1 chromatin directly, indicating indirect regulation.

Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: RVE1-OX introduced into dog1 mutant background
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Transcript increases during seed development and decreases after imbibition; repressed by active phyB
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: High in dormant seeds; reduced by red light/phyB activation
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Genetic regulation in dormancy pathway; molecular mode not resolved in this paper
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Seed dormancy program affecting GA-dependent germination output
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Major dormancy determinant acting downstream of phyB in this study
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: Mutual positive transcriptional relationship with RVE1 suggested in supplementary data
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Promotes germination when abundant; DOG1 antagonizes germination by promoting dormancy
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes primary seed dormancy downstream of phyB; RVE1 overexpression can restore dormancy in dog1 mutant background
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. DOG1 acts genetically downstream of phyB in primary seed dormancy. 2. DOG1 expression pattern parallels RVE1/RVE2 during seed development and imbibition. 3. Active phyB represses DOG1 transcript accumulation. 4. RVE1 overexpression complemented the non-dormant phenotype of dog1 mutant seeds.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Genetic analysis with dog1 mutant, dog1/phyB and dog1/RVE1-OX; qRT-PCR
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: Public expression data referenced; accession NA
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: White light; darkness; imbibition; red light/phyB activation
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: A major dormancy locus useful for tuning seed dormancy traits
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Excess DOG1 activity can impose excessive dormancy and poor field emergence
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included as a core gene because it is directly examined genetically in Results, although its direct metabolic target is not defined here.
